title: LG monitors silently install software through Windows Update without consent
summary:
LG monitors are silently installing software, primarily promoting McAfee subscriptions, through Windows Update without user consent. Gamers Nexus reproduced this behavior with an LG UltraGear 34GX900A-B, observing that Windows Update first installed LG extension and software components, followed by the LG Monitor App Installer. This installer then displayed a McAfee promotion offering a 30-day trial in 31 out of 32 tested system boots; the remaining boot promoted an LG utility.

This issue isn't limited to new displays; Gamers Nexus also received pop-ups on a three-year-old LG UltraFine 32UN880-B, with user complaints dating back to at least 2024. The installed application gains access to the internet and all system resources.

This type of automatic software installation isn't exclusive to LG; Dell uses a similar method to deliver Alienware Command Center via Windows Update for compatible hardware.

Users can block these unwanted installations by enabling the "Prevent automatic download of applications associated with device metadata" Group Policy setting found under Computer Configuration, Administrative Templates, System and Device Installation. However, this will also prevent other potentially useful monitor or peripheral software from installing automatically, requiring manual downloads.
chinese_title: LG显示器通过 Windows 更新静默安装软件，未经同意。
chinese_summary:
LG 显示器正在未经用户同意的情况下，通过 Windows Update 静默安装软件，主要推广 McAfee 订阅服务。Gamers Nexus 使用一台 LG UltraGear 34GX900A-B 重现了这种行为，观察到 Windows Update 首先安装了 LG 扩展和软件组件，随后是 LG 显示器应用安装程序。在 32 次系统启动测试中，该安装程序有 31 次显示了 McAfee 推广信息，提供 30 天试用；剩余的一次启动则推广了 LG 的一个实用工具。

这个问题并非仅限于新型显示器；Gamers Nexus 还在一台三年旧的 LG UltraFine 32UN880-B 上收到了弹窗，用户投诉至少可以追溯到 2024 年。所安装的应用程序会获得互联网访问权限和所有系统资源。

这种自动软件安装并非 LG 独有；戴尔也采用类似方法，通过 Windows Update 为兼容硬件分发 Alienware Command Center。

用户可以通过启用位于“计算机配置”、“管理模板”、“系统”和“设备安装”下的“阻止与设备元数据相关的应用程序自动下载”组策略设置来阻止这些不必要的安装。然而，这样做也会阻止其他可能实用的显示器或外围设备软件自动安装，需要用户手动下载。

title: Regressive JPEGs
summary:
"Regressive JPEGs" explores the progressive rendering feature of JPEG files, where images display low-resolution components first via multiple "scans." Each scan defines specific spectral ranges (DCT bins) and precision for color channels (Y, Cb, Cr), allowing for gradual detail enhancement.

The author experimented with manipulating this structure to embed multiple distinct images within a single JPEG. An initial attempt involved concatenating full JPEGs, stripping redundant markers. While this allowed the image to switch frames during partial download, decoders typically limited this to about nine scans, causing ghosting as later AC (high-frequency) scans refined previously rendered data.

To overcome these issues, the author devised a method using *only* DC (bin 0) scans for each "frame." This creates standards-compliant, low-resolution (1/16th) 'frames' without AC data, eliminating ghosting and significantly increasing the number of renderable frames—up to 90 in Chrome. This effectively packs a short "video" into a single JPEG.

Despite lacking practical application due to network-dependent timing, this technique offers novel possibilities for unconventional interactive content or "rickrolls," showcasing a creative repurposing of JPEG's progressive feature.
chinese_title: 退行JPEGs
chinese_summary:
“倒退式JPEG”探索了JPEG文件的渐进式渲染特性，即图像通过多次“扫描”首先显示低分辨率组件。每次扫描定义了颜色通道（Y、Cb、Cr）的特定频谱范围（DCT量化区）和精度，从而实现细节的逐步增强。

作者尝试操纵这种结构，将多个不同的图像嵌入到单个JPEG中。最初的尝试是连接完整的JPEG文件并去除冗余标记。虽然这使得图像在部分下载时可以切换帧，但解码器通常将此限制在大约九次扫描，从而导致重影，因为后续的AC（高频）扫描会优化先前渲染的数据。

为了克服这些问题，作者设计了一种方法，为每个“帧” *仅* 使用DC（第0量化区）扫描。这创建了符合标准、低分辨率（1/16）的“帧”，且不包含AC数据，从而消除了重影，并显著增加了可渲染帧的数量——在Chrome中可达90帧。这有效地将一个短“视频”打包到单个JPEG中。

尽管由于依赖网络计时而缺乏实际应用，但这项技术为非常规的交互式内容或“rickrolls”提供了新颖的可能性，展示了对JPEG渐进式特性的一种创造性再利用。

title: Transcribe.cpp
summary:
transcribe.cpp, launched in April 2026, is a new ggml-based transcription library aimed at solving the pains of distributing cross-platform speech-to-text applications. The author, maintainer of the Handy app, created it as a reliable, performant alternative to existing solutions like whisper.cpp and ONNX, which presented significant challenges for broad distribution and trust.

This v0.1.0 library offers a fast and accurate inference engine with extensive model support, currently covering 16 ASR families (60+ models) and growing. It features wide acceleration capabilities through Vulkan, Metal, CUDA, and TinyBLAS. A core principle is accuracy: every supported model undergoes numerical validation and WER testing to match its reference implementation, with results publicly available.

Designed to be a near drop-in replacement for whisper.cpp, transcribe.cpp can even run existing `.bin` files while offering comparable performance. Critical for real-world adoption, it provides maintainer-supported language bindings for Python, Javascript/Typescript, Rust, and ObjC/Swift. The project champions making local ASR more accessible and efficient, demonstrating faster-than-real-time transcription on various devices with state-of-the-art models.

The project acknowledges significant support from Mozilla AI (BiR program), the ggml community, Modal (for WER testing), Blacksmith (for CI/CD), and Hugging Face. The author confirms AI assistance was used in the engine's development but not in writing the project announcement itself.
chinese_title: 转录.cpp
chinese_summary:
transcribe.cpp 于2026年4月推出，是一个基于ggml的新转录库，旨在解决分发跨平台语音转文本应用时的痛点。该作者（Handy应用的维护者）创建transcribe.cpp，旨在为whisper.cpp和ONNX等现有解决方案提供一个可靠、高性能的替代品，因为这些现有方案在广泛分发和信任方面面临显著挑战。

这个v0.1.0库提供了一个快速准确的推理引擎，具有广泛的模型支持，目前涵盖16个ASR系列（60多个模型）并持续增长。它通过Vulkan、Metal、CUDA和TinyBLAS提供广泛的加速功能。准确性是其核心原则：每个受支持的模型都经过数值验证和WER测试，以匹配其参考实现，并且结果公开发布。

transcribe.cpp 被设计成 whisper.cpp 的近乎直接替代品，甚至可以运行现有的`.bin`文件，同时提供可比的性能。对于实际应用至关重要，它为Python、Javascript/Typescript、Rust和ObjC/Swift提供了维护者支持的语言绑定。该项目致力于使本地ASR更易于访问和高效，在各种设备上使用最先进的模型实现了超越实时的转录。

该项目感谢Mozilla AI（BiR项目）、ggml社区、Modal（用于WER测试）、Blacksmith（用于CI/CD）以及Hugging Face的大力支持。作者确认在引擎开发中使用了AI辅助，但项目公告本身并非由AI撰写。

title: Qwen 3.8
summary: Alibaba's Qwen is launching Qwen 3.8, a massive model with 2.4 trillion parameters, which will soon be open-weight. The developers claim it is one of the most powerful models available, comparable to leading frontier AI models, and second only to Fable 5. Users can already test the Qwen3.8-Max-Preview on Alibaba's Token Plan, Qoder, and QoderWork. The announcement encourages early adoption and innovation, providing links to the Token Plan for both international and Chinese users.
chinese_title: 通义千问 3.8
chinese_summary: 阿里巴巴通义千问即将推出参数规模达2.4万亿的巨型模型通义千问3.8，并很快将开源。开发者声称，它是目前最强大的模型之一，可与领先的前沿AI模型媲美，且仅次于Fable 5。用户现已可在阿里巴巴的Token Plan、Qoder和QoderWork平台上测试通义千问3.8-Max-预览版。该公告鼓励早期采用和创新，并提供了面向国际和中国用户的Token Plan链接。

title: The Kimi K3 Moment
summary:
The author finds Kimi K3, an open model, indistinguishable from Claude for coding tasks, matching performance, output quality, and token counts. However, Kimi K3 is significantly more affordable, with API costs being a fraction of Claude's ($3/$15 vs. $10/$50 per million tokens) and subscription plans offering far more value ($19/$39 vs. Claude's tightly metered options). The author also notes Claude's unreliability, citing the removal of Fable access from a paid plan due to economic unsustainability, a problem Kimi's tiers avoid.

The article then critiques US AI policy as an "unmitigated failure." It argues that American models are hindered by restrictions, refusing certain categories of work, while frontier-quality models like Kimi K3 and GLM 5.2 (released by Chinese labs under permissive licenses) are openly available, unregulated by the US, and outperform restricted US models on benchmarks. This policy, the author states, only constrains American customers. OpenAI, in contrast to Anthropic, appears to have navigated government scrutiny more effectively, maintaining a viable flagship model on cheaper plans.

The author predicts a future where the US government, mirroring its auto industry playbook, will regulate AI and open source through subsidies and tariffs. This would foster domestic, public-private AI models that are less competitive and leave American users without access to the best global models at fair prices. Consequently, the author sees no reason to continue paying for Claude.
chinese_title: 淇幂 K3 时刻
chinese_summary:
作者发现，Kimi K3这一开放模型在编码任务上与Claude难分伯仲，其性能、输出质量和token消耗量都旗鼓相当。然而，Kimi K3明显更实惠，API成本仅为Claude的一小部分（每百万token $3/$15 对比 $10/$50），订阅计划也提供了更大的价值（$19/$39 对比 Claude严格按量计费的选项）。作者还指出Claude的不可靠性，例如其因经济不可持续性而从付费计划中移除了Fable访问权限，而Kimi的分级定价则避免了此问题。

文章接着批评美国人工智能政策是“彻头彻尾的失败”。它认为美国模型受到限制的阻碍，拒绝执行某些类型的工作，而Kimi K3和GLM 5.2等前沿质量模型（由中国实验室在宽松许可下发布）则公开可用，不受美国监管，并在基准测试中超越受限的美国模型。作者指出，这项政策只限制了美国客户。与Anthropic形成对比的是，OpenAI似乎更有效地应对了政府审查，在更便宜的计划中维持了一个可行的旗舰模型。

作者预测，美国政府未来将效仿其汽车行业的策略，通过补贴和关税来监管人工智能和开源。这将培养竞争力较弱的国内公私合营人工智能模型，并使美国用户无法以合理价格获取全球最佳模型。因此，作者认为没有理由继续为Claude付费。

title: Kaiser nurses say AI, surveillance are making their jobs and patient care worse
summary:
Kaiser Permanente nurses report that artificial intelligence and surveillance technologies are negatively impacting patient care and increasing workplace stress. They describe being criticized and receiving poor performance scores for spending over 15 minutes on calls, despite Kaiser's claim it doesn't use "Average Handle Time" metrics. Software monitors productivity and call speed, and AI has been used to assess empathy and tone of voice, which nurses found inaccurate and unhelpful.

Nurses, like Raquel Alvarez Sanchez, detail instances where critical patient needs—such as suicidal callers or patients receiving life-altering diagnoses—require extended conversations, forcing them to prioritize patient care over avoiding reprimands. This pressure, they contend, leads to withholding advice, rushing complex calls, and increased emotional exhaustion, potentially compromising patient safety. Many calls involving multiple symptoms, chronic illnesses, or interpreters routinely exceed the implied 15-minute limit, which nurses deem unsafe.

The California Nurses Association (CNA) is making AI a key issue in upcoming contract negotiations, following past protests. While Kaiser states its AI use prioritizes patient safety with human oversight, nurses feel harassed and unsupported. Experts note that such intense surveillance increases worker stress and burnout, which can lead to mistakes in high-stakes healthcare settings. Nurses emphasize that their role requires human judgment and compassion, not the rigid efficiency models often imposed by algorithmic management, fearing that the focus on cost-cutting over care will ultimately fail patients.
chinese_title: 凯撒护士称AI、监控使其工作和患者护理恶化
chinese_summary:
Kaiser Permanente nurses report that artificial intelligence and surveillance technologies are negatively impacting patient care and increasing workplace stress. They describe being criticized and receiving poor performance scores for spending over 15 minutes on calls, despite Kaiser's claim it doesn't use "Average Handle Time" metrics. Software monitors productivity and call speed, and AI has been used to assess empathy and tone of voice, which nurses found inaccurate and unhelpful.

Nurses, like Raquel Alvarez Sanchez, detail instances where critical patient needs—such as suicidal callers or patients receiving life-altering diagnoses—require extended conversations, forcing them to prioritize patient care over avoiding reprimands. This pressure, they contend, leads to withholding advice, rushing complex calls, and increased emotional exhaustion, potentially compromising patient safety. Many calls involving multiple symptoms, chronic illnesses, or interpreters routinely exceed the implied 15-minute limit, which nurses deem unsafe.

The California Nurses Association (CNA) is making AI a key issue in upcoming contract negotiations, following past protests. summary: Gleam, a language designed for building type-safe and scalable systems, has launched on Tangled. The article emphasizes that Gleam is not corporately owned but relies on the generous support of its sponsors. The team expresses deep gratitude for all support.
